A MORTGAGOR IS REQUIRED TO CLEAR HIS DEBT BEFORE HE CAN CLEAR TITLE. AGUILAR V. BOCCI (1974) 39 CAL. APP. 3D 475. NO FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS ASSERTED GIVE RISE TO A CONSPIRACY AMONG DEFENDANTS TO COMMIT TORTOUS ACTS AGAINST PLAINTIFF. UPON THE WRITTEN DEED OF TRUST, DEFENDANT WAS ALLOWED TO BEGIN NON-JUDICIAL FORECLOSURE PROCEEDINGS UPON PLAINTIFF'S DEFAULT. CC 2923.5 CHALLENGE TO THE SALE IS APPLICABLE BEFORE THE FORECLOSURE SALE. MABRY V. SUPERIOR COURT (2010) 185 CAL. APP. 4TH 208. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT AURORA LOAN SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS NICHOLAS WALKER AND LARRY LOYD'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: John S. Morken, Esq. of Morken Law Office 391-6140 for pltfs & opposing parties; Alex Sears, Esq. of Severson & Werson 677-5635 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified- Defendant's demurrers to the 1st cause of action (set aside of Trustee Sale) and 5th cause of action (cancel Trustee's deed) are sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has not opposed the demurrer on those claims. Defendant's demurrer to the 2nd cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's claim that MER is not properly registered and failed to comply with California's tax laws is without merit factually and legally. Perlas v. MERS 2010 WL 3079262. Defendant's demurrer to the 3rd, 4th, 6th and 7th causes of action (fraud, negligent misrepresentation, violation of U.C.L. and Quiet Title) are sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead further facts with sufficient specificity to support these claims. Defendant's demurrer to the 8th cause of action (conspiracy) is sustained with leave to amend. Defendant properly cites Applied Equipment for the proposition that conspiracy is not a distinct cause of action. Plaintiff should re-plead the conspiracy allegations as a general charging allegation. Defendant's demurrer to the 9th cause of action (unjust enrichment) is sustained without leave to amend. Defendant properly cites McBride for the proposition that it is not a distinct cause of action. The demurrer to the 10th cause of action (TRO/injunctive relief). A request for a TRO is a remedy, not a cause of action. Because the prayer for relief seeks a TRO, this cause of action is surplusage. In addition, the Court indicated that plaintiff has 20 days leave to amend. Notice waived. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Oct-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS' ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. No appearances on this matter. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied, plaintiffs fail to show a likelihood of success on the merits. Plaintiffs are required to tender the amount owed before maintaining any cause of action for irregularity in the sale procedure. See Abdallah v. United Savings Bank (1996) 43 Cal. App. 4th 1101. A mortgagor is required to clear his debt before he can clear title. Aguilar v. Bocci (1974) 39 Cal. App. 3d 475. No factual allegations asserted give rise to a conspiracy among defendants to commit tortous acts against plaintiff. Upon the written deed of trust, defendant was allowed to begin non-judicial foreclosure proceedings upon plaintiff's default. CC 2923.5 challenge to the sale is applicable before the foreclosure sale. Mabry v. Superior Court (2010) 185 Cal. App. 4th 208. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
